title: Verify service-side delivery to the normalized Codex webhook endpoint on port 8848
objective: After the Codex onboarding thread normalizes its listener and registered webhook endpoint back to 8848, verify from the community/server side that the active Codex identity is in the intended group, has an active webhook subscription pointing to 8848, and that a real community-originated message produces webhook delivery evidence.

dependency.why: Do not validate server-side delivery against a stale 8788 registration.

mandatory_references.why: This defines the normalized 8848 target state you must verify against.
Confirm the actual server-side agent webhook delivery path and the evidence you should look for.
Confirm how current message routing works and avoid treating a plain-text @mention as a structured target.
Confirm the current UI still posts plain channel messages with null target/mentions unless explicitly changed.

required_work.description: Verify the active Codex identity is a member of the tested group and has an active webhook subscription whose target_url points to 8848.
From a valid community actor in the same group, create one real community-originated message/event and capture the server-side delivery evidence for the active Codex identity.
Prove whether the server emitted webhook delivery success or failure for the active Codex identity after the message.
State clearly whether the 8848-normalized Codex identity is now receiving real community webhook deliveries, and if not, stop at the first real blocker.

do_not: Do not change Codex-side onboarding code in this task.
Do not treat a plain-text @mention as proof of structured direct routing.
Do not broaden this into community feature work.
acceptance: Membership is confirmed for the tested group.
Active webhook subscription target_url points to 8848.
At least one real community-originated event produces delivery evidence for the Codex identity.
Delivery outcome is classified with evidence, not guesswork.
